preamble: This transformation describes a well-attested historical phonetic shift from a Proto-Indo-European (PIE) voiced aspirated bilabial plosive (represented as bʱ or *bʰ) to a voiceless labiodental fricative (f). This sound change is a fundamental characteristic in the development of the Italic branch of Indo-European languages, most notably in Latin, distinguishing its phonological evolution from other branches.
phoneticEffects: The shift from a voiced aspirated bilabial plosive to a voiceless labiodental fricative involves several key phonetic transformations

examples.note: Proto-Indo-European root 'to carry' to Latin 'I carry'
Proto-Indo-European root for 'trust' to Latin 'faith' (as in fīdes)

sources: Phonetic changes from PIE to Latin - Reddit
What are some sound laws that describe how PIE developed into latin? - Quora
Faliscan Reflexes of the Indo-European voiced aspirates - Wolfgang D. C. de Melo
